Ensure run_conformance.sh forwards BUILD_DIR to make build and run_profile_parity.sh writes adapter artifact under selected BUILD_DIR.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayRiver","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:33.021827010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:35.858189947Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:35.858161123Z","close_reason":"Patched tools/ci/run_conformance.sh to pass BUILD_DIR to make build, and tools/ci/run_profile_parity.sh to write adapter artifact under selected BUILD_DIR.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vt","title":"EPIC: Advanced Alpha Levers and Research Backlog (No Feature Loss)","description":"Background:
The plan includes advanced ideas (formal assurance ladder, lock-free alternatives, cache-oblivious layouts, etc.). These should be pursued via controlled spikes with rollback and proof artifacts.

Goal:
Run high-EV research tasks that can elevate performance/assurance without semantic compromise.

Constraints:
- one lever at a time
- explicit baseline/profile/isomorphism artifacts
- fallback path mandatory

Why this serves users:
- keeps project ambitious without destabilizing the core delivery path.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.632150816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850235463Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850215876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alpha","optimization","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -118,6 +120,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-66l.7","title":"Implement anti-butchering proof-block gate for semantic-sensitive changes","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.13 requires semantic-sensitive pull requests to include guarantee-substitution notes, proof artifacts, and fallback/isomorphism statements.

Subtasks:
- Define required metadata template for semantic-sensitive changes.
- Implement CI check that fails when required proof fields are missing.
- Link required fields to traceability index and artifact locations.
- Document how to request and review exceptions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Semantic-sensitive changes cannot merge without complete proof block metadata.
- Metadata links are machine-checkable and reviewer-friendly.
- Exception flow is explicit and auditable.","notes":"Verified anti-butchering proof-block enforcement is wired and auditable: Makefile lint-anti-butchering target invokes tools/ci/check_anti_butchering.sh; CI check job runs lint-anti-butchering and records manifest rerun pointers. Gate fail-path validated locally (no metadata -> exit 1 with missing fields and anti_butcher JSON artifact). Gate pass-path validated with complete Guarantee Impact metadata block (exit 0, artifact emitted). Added compatibility shim tools/ci/check_proof_block_gate.sh -> check_anti_butchering.sh and updated GUARANTEE_SUBSTITUTION_MATRIX exception flow docs with explicit non-zero budget review steps and rerun guidance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacHawk","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.340855881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:44:09.208410249Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:39:00.921139480Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","governance","quality-gates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.7","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.7","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.8","title":"Implement per-target binary-size and cold-start SLO gates","description":"Background:
Idea round 2 includes per-target binary size and cold-start gates as embedded adoption multipliers.

Subtasks:
- Capture size and cold-start baselines for key target profiles and triplets.
- Define budget policy and tolerance windows per profile/resource class.
- Integrate checks into CI with clear delta reporting and reproducible rerun commands.
- Emit structured gate artifacts (target, baseline, delta, suspect modules, run metadata) for trend analysis and release review.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Regressions in size/start metrics fail gate when outside approved budgets.
- Reports identify changed targets and likely contributing modules.
- Baseline updates require explicit rationale and audit trail.
- Gate artifacts are machine-readable and linkable from release evidence bundles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.423164995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:29:18.543969085Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:29:18.543951361Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","embedded","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.9","title":"Enforce per-bead evidence linkage contract for unit/e2e/logging completeness","description":"Background:
The hard-part verification rules require every semantic-sensitive implementation bead to carry explicit proof linkage, not only CI-level gating.

Subtasks:
- Define required evidence fields per implementation bead: linked unit tests, linked e2e scripts, expected artifact manifests, and replay/parity references.
- Require linkage against the unit/invariant completeness matrix (bd-1md.12) and canonical e2e suite contract (bd-1md.10) so evidence remains coverage-complete, not ad hoc.
- Add validation tooling that checks bead metadata and notes for required evidence references before status transitions to done.
- Integrate checks with anti-butchering proof-block gate and traceability index updates.
- Provide reviewer/operator guidance for interpreting evidence packs quickly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Semantic-sensitive beads cannot be marked complete without linked unit/e2e/logging evidence.
- Evidence packs reference explicit unit/invariant obligations and at least one canonical e2e lane per covered semantic surface.
- Validation failures are explicit and actionable.
- Evidence links remain machine-checkable for audit and release workflows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralValley","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:18.357456678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:44:38.432684259Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:44:38.432665053Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","quality-gates","testing","traceability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-296","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-296.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-6vw","title":"Emit task_transition trace events for runtime task state changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:03:48.257372639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:06:59.768590994Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:06:59.763213603Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hwb","title":"EPIC: Core Semantics and Guarantee Substitution in ANSI C/C99","description":"Background:
Rust compile-time guarantees are absent in C. We must reconstitute equivalent runtime/proof guarantees via explicit mechanisms.

Goal:
Implement asx_core semantic primitives and safety scaffolding with deterministic failure behavior.

Core obligations:
- explicit state authorities
- generation-safe handles and ownership boundaries
- ghost monitors and linearity checks
- strict resource exhaustion semantics
- canonical error taxonomy and stability policy

Why this serves users:
- correctness under pressure for embedded systems
- predictable failure semantics for high-stakes systems
- confidence that performance tuning does not erase guarantees.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.038789705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:59:25.155834562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:59:25.155810537Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","phase-3","phase-4","safety","semantics"]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.1","title":"Implement core API types: status, errors, outcomes, budgets, cancellation, and IDs","description":"Background:
These primitives are shared across all runtime behavior and therefore must be stable, deterministic, and audit-friendly.

Subtasks:
- Define status/error taxonomy with stable code families.
- Define outcome types and ordering semantics.
- Define budget and cancellation core types with explicit failure semantics.
- Define ID formats and generation strategy for handle safety.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Types are fully documented and covered by unit tests.
- Error/status semantics are deterministic and profile-agnostic.
- Public API naming and visibility are consistent.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.812710740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:10:04.652897529Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:10:04.652875979Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","core","phase-3","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.10","title":"Implement protothread-style task poll contract and region-arena captured-state safety","description":"Background:
Plan sections 6.8.A and 7.4 require a safe C replacement for async/await that preserves lifecycle semantics without stack-lifetime footguns.

Subtasks:
- Implement coroutine-style task macros (`ASX_CO_BEGIN`, `ASX_CO_YIELD`, `ASX_CO_END`) with explicit resume state.
- Enforce region-arena ownership of captured task state; no malloc-owned suspended state in kernel profiles.
- Add explicit yield-safety lint gate that flags non-const locals/raw internal pointers persisting across yield unless waived.
- Define spawn contract for state sizing/finalization so suspended tasks cannot leak on region close.
- Add tests for suspend/resume ordering, cancellation checkpoints, and region-close cleanup.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Poll contract is deterministic and fixture-tested.
- No leaked task state on cancellation or region closure.
- Yield-safety violations are caught automatically by CI lint/static checks.
- Macro workflow is documented with explicit do/dont examples.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:56:45.639588066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:08:14.924460519Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:08:14.924440612Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","coroutines","hard-part"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -153,5 +156,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-j4m.6","title":"Create deployment hardening playbooks and golden domain scenario packs","description":"Background:
High-quality deployment requires repeatable operational guidance and domain-representative workload packs, backed by deterministic e2e evidence.

Subtasks:
- Create router-storm, market-open burst, and automotive fault-burst scenario packs.
- Integrate matching vertical e2e execution scripts (via shared harness) with structured logs, manifests, and rerun commands.
- Document watchdog/restart/safe-upgrade guidance per profile.
- Define low-overhead remote diagnostics framing for constrained links.
- Map each playbook lane to required unit/invariant obligations and parity evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scenario packs are executable in CI and local environments.
- Playbooks include clear operator runbooks and rollback guidance.
- Guidance ties directly to profile behavior guarantees and limits.
- Field triage can be performed from captured artifacts without ad-hoc instrumentation.
- Vertical playbook lanes include explicit links to e2e manifests and supporting unit/invariant evidence.","notes":"Completed deployment-hardening lane closure: verified scenario packs executable via rch-offloaded make test-e2e (PASS) and full gates (build/format-check/lint/test/conformance PASS). Integrated deploy lanes into Makefile E2E_ALL_SCRIPTS/test-e2e coverage (router_storm, market_open_burst, automotive_fault_burst). Added cross-profile deployment playbook guidance in docs/SAFETY_PROFILES.md: watchdog/restart/safe-upgrade procedures, low-overhead remote diagnostics framing, and explicit lane->unit/invariant/parity evidence linkage matrix. Existing lane scripts/tests: tests/e2e/{router_storm,market_open_burst,automotive_fault_burst}.sh + corresponding e2e_*.c and run_all gate mapping.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacHawk","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.121422340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:29:14.072530918Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:27:16.399699014Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","playbook","profiles","scenarios"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-j4m.7","title":"Implement OpenWrt packaging kit and reproducible install artifacts","description":"Background:
Plan identifies OpenWrt packaging as a practical multiplier for router adoption.

Subtasks:
- Build OpenWrt package recipe and dependency-free install flow.
- Verify package across router-class target matrix and QEMU smoke.
- Add package-focused e2e scripts (install/upgrade/rollback/startup sanity) with structured logs and manifests.
- Document package upgrade/rollback handling, compatibility caveats, and artifact troubleshooting flow.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Package builds reproducibly and installs cleanly on supported targets.
- Smoke and package-e2e tests pass with profile/resource-class defaults.
- Packaging docs are sufficient for operator self-service.
- Artifact logs/manifests provide actionable diagnosis for package failures.","notes":"Implemented OpenWrt packaging kit: recipe scaffold (packaging/openwrt/asx/Makefile), init hook (packaging/openwrt/asx/files/asx.init), deterministic builder (tools/packaging/openwrt/build_package.sh), package e2e lane (tests/e2e/openwrt_package.sh), run_all gate registration (GATE-E2E-PACKAGE), and operator docs (docs/OPENWRT_PACKAGING.md). Builder now supports ASX_USE_RCH=1|0|auto and offloads make build via rch in auto/forced mode. Validation: ASX_USE_RCH=1 tests/e2e/openwrt_package.sh => 8/8 passed with deterministic checksum stability; full gates via rch passed: make build, format-check, lint, test, conformance.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacHawk","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:19.923781998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:18:16.473682671Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:18:16.473658426Z","close_reason":"Completed: OpenWrt packaging scaffold + deterministic artifact builder + package e2e gate + docs implemented and validated via rch-offloaded quality gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","openwrt","packaging","profiles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.7","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.7","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-j4m.8","title":"Create per-profile deterministic overload policy catalog with formal fixture coverage","description":"Background:
Idea round 3 calls for a per-profile deterministic overload policy catalog with formal fixture coverage to prevent drift.

Subtasks:
- Define overload policy catalog for core, embedded_router, hft, and automotive profiles.
- Specify deterministic outcomes and degraded-mode transitions for each overload class.
- Map each policy row to fixture IDs and parity gates.
- Document forbidden behavior patterns and fallback requirements.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Catalog is complete, versioned, and machine-checkable.
- Every overload policy has explicit fixture coverage and expected outcomes.
- Policy changes require linked artifact updates and review rationale.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:58:52.896778117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T23:09:58.896720728Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T23:09:58.896702594Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","governance","profiles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.8","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.8","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
